title: 10 best herbal tinctures for toothache
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for toothache are concentrated liquid extracts of herbs, which are used to treat toothache by providing natural pain relief and reducing inflammation. Examples of such herbs include clove, which contains eugenol, a natural anesthetic, and anti-inflammatory agent, and calendula, which has antiseptic and analgesic properties. These tinctures can be taken sublingually (under the tongue) or added to water for gargling. By using herbal tinctures, people can find relief from toothache and avoid the side effects of over-the-counter painkillers. This can improve their quality of life, especially for those who prefer natural remedies or have allergies or sensitivities to conventional medications.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow tincture helps with toothache because it contains salicin, a compound that has similar pain-relieving properties as aspirin. Salicin works by reducing inflammation and swelling in the affected area, which can help alleviate the pain and discomfort caused by a toothache. Additionally, white willow tincture has natural analgesic and antiseptic properties that can help soothe irritation and promote healing in the mouth. Its anti-inflammatory effects can also help reduce any fever or swelling associated with a toothache. Overall, white willow tincture can provide natural and effective relief for toothache symptoms.
remedy_properties: Analgesic property: White willow tincture contains salicin, which is a natural pain reliever, helping to alleviate the pain associated with toothache.
Anti-inflammatory property: The salicin in white willow tincture reduces inflammation, thereby decreasing swelling and pain in the affected tooth and surrounding tissues.
Antipyretic property: White willow tincture helps reduce fever, which is often associated with toothache, by slowing the production of prostaglandins, chemicals that cause inflammation and fever.
Antimicrobial property: White willow tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which can contribute to toothache and dental infections.

Anesthetic property: White willow tincture has a mild anesthetic effect, which can help numb the pain and discomfort associated with toothache. This effect is due to the salicin and other compounds found in the bark of the white willow tree.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the white willow tree is commonly used to make medicinal tea for toothache due to its high salicin content, which acts as a natural pain reliever.
Leaves: The leaves of the white willow plant are used in tea preparation for toothache relief, as they contain flavonoids that increase the anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects of salicin.

Rhyzomes: White willow rhyzomes are used in medicinal tea for toothache because they store nutrients and contain active compounds, such as salicin and flavonoids, that help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of white willow bark into small pieces and combine with 2 cups of vodka or rum in a clean jar.
Seal the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the designated time, strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer into a clean jar or bottle.
Discard the solid remains and store the liquid in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
To use, dilute 1-2 mL of the tincture in water or another liquid and use it as needed for toothache relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with toothache because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the affected area. The herb, Achillea millefolium, from which yarrow tincture is derived, has been used traditionally for dental issues due to its astringent and antimicrobial properties that can help to reduce infection and promote healing. Yarrow tincture can help to numb the pain and provide relief from toothache, making it a popular natural remedy for dental discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and pain in the gums and tooth, thanks to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.
Analgesic: Yarrow tincture can alleviate toothache by numbing the affected area, due to the active constituents like alkaloids and salicylic acid.
Antispasmodic: It can help relax muscle spasms and ease the pain caused by tooth grinding or clenching, owing to the presence of lactones and essential oils.
Antimicrobial: Yarrow tincture can help fight tooth infections and inhibit bacterial growth, which is crucial for toothache relief, as it contains antimicrobial compounds like azulenes and lactones.
Astringent: Its astringent property helps reduce inflammation and tighten gum tissue, promoting healing and reducing pain, due to the tannins found in yarrow.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce toothache and swelling.
Leaves: Rich in analgesic compounds, providing pain relief for toothaches.
Stems: Have astringent properties that can help reduce gum inflammation and alleviate tooth pain.
Roots: Contain antimicrobial agents that can help fight tooth infections causing toothaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, chop finely, and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the chopped herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the plant material.
Store the finished yarrow tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, in a cool, dark place for up to a year. For toothache, use 5-10 drops of the tincture every few hours as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with toothache because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the affected area. The active compounds in St. John's wort, such as hypericin and hyperforin, can help to numb the pain and promote healing. Additionally, St. John's wort has been shown to have antibacterial properties, which can help to fight off any infection that may be contributing to the toothache. When applied directly to the affected tooth and surrounding gum tissue, St. John's wort tincture can provide rapid and effective relief from toothache symptoms.
remedy_properties: Analgesic: St. John's wort tincture has pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate the discomfort caused by a toothache, due to the presence of hypericin and hyperforin.

Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory effects can reduce swelling and inflammation in the affected area, which is important for toothache as it often involves inflammation around the tooth or in the gums, thanks to hypericin and hyperforin.

Antimicrobial: St. John's wort tincture can help fight bacterial infections that may cause toothache, as it contains antimicrobial compounds that can inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria, such as hyperforin.

Anxiolytic: The calming effects of St. John's wort tincture can help reduce anxiety and tension associated with toothache, making it easier to manage the pain, due to the presence of hypericin and other active constituents.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of St. John's wort tincture can help protect tissues from damage and promote healing, which is important for toothache as it can help accelerate the recovery process and reduce the risk of complications, thanks to the flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the tincture.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in hypericin and hyperforin, St. John's Wort flowers help reduce inflammation and pain, making them ideal for toothache tea.
Leaves: High in tannins and antioxidants, St. John's Wort leaves help soothe irritated oral tissues and alleviate toothache symptoms.

Aerial parts: A combination of above-ground components, including leaves, flowers, and buds, can provide a synergistic effect for treating toothache due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully open and have not been exposed to rain for at least 24 hours.
Chop or crush the herbs to increase their surface area, then fill a clean jar 3/4 full with the chopped herbs.
Pour a 1:1 ratio of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) and filtered water over the herbs until they are completely submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar gently every few days.

Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid, and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Use 1-2 dropperfuls of the tincture every 2-3 hours as needed for toothache relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tincture helps with toothache because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the affected area. The herb's mucilage content can also form a protective layer over the damaged tooth or gum, providing further relief. Additionally, plantain has antibacterial properties that can help fight off any infection causing the toothache. Its astringent properties can also help reduce bleeding and promote healing in the mouth. Overall, plantain tincture can be a natural and effective remedy for toothache.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture helps reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, providing relief from toothache caused by inflammation, due to the presence of flavonoids and iridoid glycosides.
Analgesic: Plantain tincture alleviates pain associated with toothache by numbing the affected area, thanks to the presence of tannins and mucilage.
Antibacterial: Plantain tincture inhibits bacterial growth in the mouth, reducing the risk of infection and inflammation, due to the presence of allantoin and tannins.
Astringent: Plantain tincture tightens and strengthens gum tissue, promoting healing and reducing toothache, as a result of the tannins and mucilage.
Antioxidant: Plantain tincture neutralizes free radicals in the mouth, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall oral health, due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the plantain tincture plant have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in alleviating toothache.
Seeds: The seeds of the plantain tincture plant contain mucilage, which can help soothe inflamed gums and provide relief from toothache.
Bark: The bark of the plantain tincture plant has astringent properties, making it useful for reducing swelling and pain in the gums and teeth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest plantain leaves from a healthy, pesticide-free source, choosing ones that are fully grown and free from damage.
Rinse the leaves thoroughly, then roughly chop them and fill a clean jar, leaving about an inch of space at the top.
Pour 80-proof vodka or rum over the leaves until they are completely submerged, then securely fasten the lid and let the mixture steep for 4-6 weeks in a cool, dark place.
Shake the jar gently every few days to ensure even extraction.

After the steeping period, strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, squeezing out any excess liquid, and store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Use a dropper to apply a few drops of the tincture directly to the affected tooth as needed for pain relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tincture helps with toothache because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the affected area. Comfrey contains allantoin, a substance that promotes healing and can help to repair damaged tissue in the mouth. Additionally, its astringent properties can help to reduce bleeding and soothe irritated gums. When used as a mouthwash or applied directly to the affected tooth, comfrey tincture can provide rapid relief from toothache and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to allantoin and rosmarinic acid, help reduce swelling and pain in the gums and mouth.
Analgesic: The analgesic effects of comfrey tincture, attributed to its tannins and pyrrolizidine alkaloids, can alleviate toothache by numbing the affected area.
Antimicrobial: Comfrey tincture's antimicrobial properties, from rosmarinic acid and tannins, can help combat bacterial infections causing toothaches.
Astringent: The astringent qualities of comfrey tincture, owing to tannins, can help contract inflamed gums and tissues, reducing pain and promoting healing.
Cell proliferation: Comfrey tincture's ability to promote cell proliferation, mainly due to allantoin, can speed up the healing process of damaged gums and tissues in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots contain allantoin, a compound known to help heal tissues, making them an effective addition to a medicinal tea for toothache.
Leaves: Comfrey leaves are rich in rosmarinic acid and other anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help alleviate toothache symptoms when used in a medicinal tea.
Flowers: Comfrey flowers contain mucilage, which can help soothe inflamed tissues and provide relief from toothache when consumed as a medicinal tea.
remedy_recipe: Chop fresh comfrey roots and leaves into small pieces, filling a clean, dry mason jar up to 3/4 full.
Pour 80-proof vodka over the herbs, ensuring they are completely submerged, and seal the jar tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently once a day.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out the excess liquid from the herbs.
Label the bottle with the preparation date and store it in a cool, dark place; apply 2-3 drops of the tincture directly to the affected tooth as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kinnikinnick tincture helps with toothache because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the affected area. The herb's active compounds, such as tannins and essential oils, work together to numb the pain and soothe irritated gums. Additionally, kinnikinnick has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off any infection causing the toothache, promoting faster healing and providing long-lasting relief. Its astringent properties can also reduce bleeding and help tissue repair, making it an effective natural remedy for toothache.
remedy_properties: Analgesic: Kinnikinnick tincture contains salicylates, which act as a natural pain reliever, helping to alleviate the pain caused by a toothache.
Anti-inflammatory: The tincture has anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of tannins, which can reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums and mouth, providing relief from toothache.

Antiseptic: Kinnikinnick tincture contains compounds with antiseptic properties, such as terpenes, that can help kill bacteria and prevent infection, promoting dental health and reducing toothache symptoms.

Astringent: The tincture's astringent properties, attributed to tannins, can help tighten and contract tissues, reducing bleeding and providing relief from painful toothaches caused by gum inflammation or injury.
Anesthetic: Kinnikinnick tincture contains anesthetic compounds, such as lignans, that can help numb the affected area, providing temporary relief from toothache pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain high levels of antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in alleviating toothache pain.
Bark: Rich in tannins, the bark helps reduce inflammation and can numb the affected area, providing relief from toothache.
Leaves: Packed with antibacterial and astringent properties, leaves can help reduce infection and swelling in the oral cavity.
Flowers: High in antioxidants and flavonoids, flowers aid in reducing inflammation and soothing irritated gums and teeth.
remedy_recipe: Gather equal parts of dried bark from dogwood, oak, and willow trees, as well as crushed corn kernel and dried mint leaves, resulting in a total of two ounces of herbal mixture.
Place the herbal mixture in a clean glass jar, then pour 10 fluid ounces of 80-proof vodka or rum over it, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking the jar gently every few days.
After the designated time, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Label the bottle with the contents, date, and recommended dosage (typically 20-30 drops, three times a day), and store it in a cool, dark place for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Quaking aspen tincture helps with toothache because it contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that has natural pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied directly to the affected tooth or gum, it can help reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote healing. Additionally, aspen has antibacterial and antiviral properties that can help fight off infection, making it an effective natural remedy for toothaches caused by dental issues or viral infections. Its soothing and calming effects can also help alleviate the discomfort and anxiety associated with tooth pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Quaking aspen tincture can reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, helping to alleviate toothache caused by inflammation, due to the presence of salicin and other anti-inflammatory compounds.
Analgesic: The salicin in quaking aspen tincture acts as a natural painkiller, helping to relieve the pain associated with toothache.
Antimicrobial: Quaking aspen tincture has antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat toothache caused by bacterial infections, due to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of quaking aspen tincture can help to reduce damage to gum tissue and promote healing, which can help to alleviate toothache, due to the presence of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and other antioxidant compounds.

Astringent: Quaking aspen tincture has astringent properties, which can help to reduce swelling and bleeding in the gums, helping to alleviate toothache caused by gum disease or injury, due to the presence of tannins.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the quaking aspen contains salicin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate toothache.
Buds: The buds of the quaking aspen are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for toothache relief.
Leaves: The leaves of the quaking aspen contain tannins, which have astringent properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth.
Flowers: The flowers of the quaking aspen contain essential oils and bioflavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them helpful for treating toothache.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the bark: Gather young, green bark from the trunk of a quaking aspen tree (Populus tremuloides) in spring or early summer, peeling it off in thin strips.
Chop and measure: Finely chop the bark and measure out 1 cup (75 grams) to be used in the tincture.
Combine ingredients: Place the chopped bark in a clean jar, adding 2 cups (473 ml) of 100-proof vodka or other high-proof alcohol, ensuring the bark is fully submerged.
Steep and strain: Allow the mixture to steep for 4-6 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking it occasionally; then strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, discarding the spent bark.

Store and use: Keep the finished aspen bark tincture in a tightly sealed glass container, storing it in a cool, dark location; apply several drops of the tincture directly to the affected tooth or gums as needed for toothache relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Redosier dogwood tincture helps with toothache because it contains active compounds that have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds work to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the affected area, providing relief from the discomfort of a toothache. Additionally, redosier dogwood has been traditionally used to treat dental issues due to its astringent properties, which can help to reduce swelling and promote healing. The tincture form allows for quick absorption, providing fast-acting relief for toothache symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Redosier dogwood tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with toothache, thanks to the presence of tannins and flavonoids.
Analgesic: The tincture can alleviate toothache pain due to its analgesic properties, which are attributed to the presence of cornin and other sesquiterpene derivatives.
Antimicrobial: Redosier dogwood tincture can help fight toothache-causing bacteria, as it has antimicrobial properties, attributed mainly to its tannin content.
Astringent: Its astringent properties, due to tannins, can help reduce inflammation, protect damaged tissue, and relieve pain in the mouth and gums.

Circulatory stimulant: The tincture can improve blood flow in the affected area, promoting healing and reducing pain, due to its circulatory stimulant properties, attributed to the presence of cornin and other sesquiterpene derivatives.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the redosier dogwood contains tannins and corilagin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate toothache.
Twigs: The twigs of the redosier dogwood contain salicin, a compound that can help reduce pain and inflammation in the mouth and gums.
Berries: The berries of the redosier dogwood contain antioxidants and are used to treat mouth and throat infections, which can contribute to toothache.
Leaves: The leaves of the redosier dogwood contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help relieve toothache.
remedy_recipe: Harvest redosier dogwood (Cornus sericea) bark in late winter or early spring, choosing branches that are at least 1/2 inch in diameter.
Chop or grind the bark to increase its surface area, then fill a clean jar halfway with the bark pieces.
Pour 80-proof vodka over the bark until it is completely submerged, then secure the jar with a lid and let it steep for 4-6 weeks in a cool, dark place.
Shake the jar gently every few days to help extract the medicinal compounds from the bark.

After 4-6 weeks, strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean jar or bottle, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the bark. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use a few drops on the affected tooth or gums as needed for toothache relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Staghorn sumac tincture helps with toothache because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the affected area. The herb's active compounds, including tannins and gallic acid, work together to numb the pain and soothe irritated gums. Additionally, staghorn sumac has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infection and promote healing, making it a popular natural remedy for toothaches and other oral health issues. Its astringent properties can also help to reduce swelling and bleeding in the mouth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Staghorn sumac tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for toothache as it can alleviate pain and swelling caused by inflammation in the affected area. This property is attributed to the presence of tannins and flavonoids.

Analgesic: The tincture has pain-relieving properties, which is crucial for toothache as it can help manage the pain until a dental professional can be consulted. The analgesic effect is primarily due to the presence of tannins.

Antimicrobial: Staghorn sumac tincture inhibits bacterial growth, which is important for toothache as it can help combat infections that may cause tooth pain. The antimicrobial properties are attributed to the presence of gallic acid and other phenolic compounds.

Astringent: The tincture can help reduce secretions and promote tissue contraction, which is important for toothache as it can help alleviate symptoms caused by excess saliva or inflammation. The astringent properties are primarily due to the presence of tannins.

Antioxidant: Staghorn sumac tincture has antioxidant properties, which is important for toothache as it can help reduce oxidative stress and promote healing in the affected area. The antioxidant properties are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other phenolic compounds.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the staghorn sumac is rich in tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and provide relief from toothache.
Leaves: The leaves of the staghorn sumac contain antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for treating toothache.
Berries: The berries of the staghorn sumac have astringent properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate toothache.
Roots: The roots of the staghorn sumac contain compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for treating toothache.
Stems: The stems of the staghorn sumac contain tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and provide relief from toothache.
remedy_recipe: Harvest: Pick ripe staghorn sumac berries, ensuring they have turned a reddish-purple color and feel soft to the touch.
Prepare: Rinse the berries gently in cool water, then remove the seeds and pulp, leaving only the outer husks.
Measure: Fill a clean jar with the husks, leaving about an inch of space at the top, and add 80-proof vodka until the husks are fully submerged.
Steep: Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.

Strain: After steeping, strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, discarding the husks and storing the liquid in a tightly sealed glass container. Use 1-2 dropperfuls as needed for toothache relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black cherry tincture helps with toothache because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the mouth and gums. The active compounds in black cherries, such as anthocyanins and other flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation. Additionally, black cherry tincture has antioxidant properties that can help protect the teeth and gums from damage caused by free radicals and other harmful substances. By reducing inflammation and providing pain relief, black cherry tincture can help ease the discomfort and pain associated with toothaches, making it a popular natural remedy for oral health issues.
remedy_properties: Analgesic: Black cherry tincture contains compounds such as anthocyanins and phenolic acids, which have pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate toothache.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of black cherry tincture, due to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and anthocyanins, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums and mouth, thus providing relief from toothache.

Antibacterial: Black cherry tincture has antibacterial properties, thanks to compounds like anthocyanins and tannins, which help fight bacterial infections causing tooth decay and thus help with toothache.
Astringent: The astringent properties of black cherry tincture, due to the presence of tannins, help reduce inflammation, tighten gum tissues, and relieve pain caused by toothache.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content of black cherry tincture, owing to anthocyanins and other polyphenols, helps protect gum tissues from oxidative stress and damage, promoting oral health and assisting in toothache relief.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the black cherry tree contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate toothache pain.
Buds: The buds of the black cherry tree are rich in antioxidants and can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and gums.
Flowers: The flowers of the black cherry tree have analgesic properties that can help relieve toothache pain.
Seeds: The seeds of the black cherry tree contain essential oils that have antiseptic properties, which can help prevent tooth decay and gum disease.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 cups of black cherry tree bark into small pieces, place in a clean jar, then add 2 cups of 100-proof vodka.
Tightly seal the jar and let it steep in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After steeping, strain the mixture using cheesecloth into a clean jar, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Discard the solid remains and store the liquid in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
For toothache relief, dilute the tincture with an equal amount of water and use a dropper to place a few drops under the tongue, repeating as needed every 15-30 minutes.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat toothache with herbal tinctures is by using a tincture of clove oil, a potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic. Dilute one or two drops of clove oil tincture in a half teaspoon of warm water and swish it around the affected tooth. Alternatively, apply a drop or two of the undiluted tincture directly on the affected tooth using a cotton swab. Repeat this process every few hours or as needed. Additionally, tinctures of calendula, goldenseal, or St. John's wort can be used as a mouthwash to promote healing and reduce pain and inflammation. However, it's crucial to consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment of toothache.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to toothache that are treated with herbal tinctures include gum inflammation, mouth ulcers, and sore throats. For gum inflammation, anti-inflammatory and antibacterial herbs like calendula, goldenseal, and myrrh can be used. For mouth ulcers, tinctures made from licorice root, echinacea, and chamomile can provide relief due to their soothing and healing properties. In the case of sore throats, herbs such as sage, wild indigo, and echinacea can be used in tincture form to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation.
